overview: The witest28-2 system is a minimal single-component architecture consisting of one Go backend service that serves a rendered Hello World HTML page. The service handles all routing, security headers, error pages, health checks, and structured request logging — eliminating the need for a separate frontend component. All traffic flows directly from the public internet to this single Dockerized service running on Kubernetes.
requirements: The root URL (/) must return HTTP 200 OK with an HTML page prominently displaying 'Hello, World!'
The page must have a valid HTML structure with a descriptive <title> tag (e.g. 'Hello World') in the <head>
The page must be responsive and render correctly on desktop and mobile across Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge (latest 2 major versions)
The page must meet basic accessibility standards: semantic HTML and sufficient color contrast
Unrecognized routes must return HTTP 404 with a user-friendly error page
Internal server errors must return HTTP 500 with a user-friendly error page that does not expose stack traces or file paths
The /health endpoint must return HTTP 200 with JSON body {"status": "ok"}
All responses must include HTTP security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, Referrer-Policy, and Strict-Transport-Security
The application must not expose server version information in any response headers or error output
All requests must be logged with timestamp, HTTP method, path, status code, and response latency
All unhandled server errors must be logged internally for diagnostics
The application must respond within 500ms under normal load
The application must target 99% monthly uptime via Kubernetes deployment with appropriate replica and health check configuration

components.name: hello-world-service

Hello, World!

" '404': description: Not Found — user-friendly error page content: text/html: schema: type: string '500': description: Internal Server Error — user-friendly error page content: text/html: schema: type: string /health: get: summary: Health check description: Returns service health status. responses: '200': description: Service is healthy content: application/json: schema: type: object properties: status: type: string example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions:
## Implementation: hello-world-service ### Language & Framework - **Language:** Go (1.22+) - **HTTP library:** standard `net/http` — no third-party web frameworks - **Port:** `9090` ### Project Structure ``` /hello-world-service main.go Dockerfile go.mod ``` ### Routing Register all routes on a single `http.ServeMux`: | Method | Path | Handler | |--------|------|--------| | GET | `/` | serveIndex | | GET | `/health` | serveHealth | | * | everything else | serve404 | Use an explicit catch-all: if the path is not `/` or `/health`, call the 404 handler. ### Middleware Wrap the mux with two middleware layers (applied in order): 1. **Security headers middleware** — adds the following headers to *every* response before writing: - `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` - `X-Frame-Options: DENY` - `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'` - `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer` - `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` - Remove the `Server` header entirely (set it to empty string). 2. **Logging middleware** — wraps a custom `ResponseWriter` that captures the status code; after the handler returns, logs a single structured line to stdout: ``` {"time":"","method":"GET","path":"/","status":200,"latency_ms":3} ``` ### Handlers #### `serveIndex` - Respond with `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8` and status `200`. - Return a complete, valid HTML5 document: - `Hello World` in ``. - A `` tag for responsiveness. - In ``, a semantic `
` element containing an `

Hello, World!

`. - Inline `
